threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction, not just Junction. The locals dropped the 'Dalston' ages ago.
Last night on the 59, a guy tried to sell a half-eaten kebab. Said it was 'vintage'—only in Brixton, right? Everyone just laughed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found myself discussing the best döner spots with my neighbor while the kids played in the courtyard. That's when it felt real.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a kebab shop that doubles as a hipster bar – complete with craft beer on tap right next to the döner grill.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their relaxed smile while dodging a dog on the Muni tracks, not panicking like the tourists. Happens all too often.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 at Civic Center just as a guy in a tutu hopped on with a cat in his arms. Only in SF, right? Never a dull ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just La Rambla with a hipster twist, but the real heart beats in the quiet squares after dark, where locals gather over cheap wine.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look confused when the metro doors close behind them, while the locals just glide through like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground'—those tracks are holy in Dalston.
Caught a late 333 last week; the driver stopped for a kebab run at 2am. Best part? He yelled 'order up!' as he walked back on. Pure Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football in the middle of the street while an old man sold fresh pretzels from his bike. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with street art and BYO snacks—it’s an underground vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know to carry an umbrella even when the skies are clear—just wait for that 4 PM drizzle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that had a break dance battle in the aisle—only in the Tenderloin would that happen at 8 AM. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals dine at 9 PM. Meanwhile, we’re already finished and chatting over coffee by then.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with their T-10 tickets, swiping through apps instead of just asking a local for directions.
